skill.name: ai-research-explore
skill.description: Plan and audit candidate-only deep learning research exploration from an existing current_research anchor. The skill coordinates source lookup, idea gating, feasibility checks, and experiment artifacts for bounded Rigor Explore campaigns.
skill.summary: Coordinate bounded deep learning research exploration with auditable candidate ranking and experiment artifacts.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: AI review found no prompt-injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent, and many static hits are markdown backticks or non-security SHA1 digests. The skill still has legitimate but elevated surfaces: subprocess orchestration, git branch and worktree mutation, filesystem artifact writes, public HTTP source lookup, and optional environment variable checks.

medium_findings.title: Local Command Execution for Research Orchestration
medium_findings.description: The orchestration code runs Python pass scripts and git commands through subprocess, including branch and worktree operations. Calls use argument lists rather than shell=True, so command injection evidence was not found, but the skill can mutate the local repository and execute bundled scripts when authorized.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess calls and git worktree creation are present. The arguments are structured lists and no shell=True evidence was found, so the risk is operational rather than confirmed malicious.

medium_findings.title: Hardware Reconnaissance Commands
medium_findings.description: The feasibility pass runs nvidia-smi and rocm-smi to detect local accelerator resources. This is relevant for experiment planning, but it discloses local hardware characteristics to generated reports and should be disclosed to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands and platform reporting are directly visible. The context is feasibility analysis, not stealth reconnaissance, which keeps confidence below very high.

medium_findings.title: Bounded Public Network Lookup
medium_findings.description: Lookup providers fetch public metadata from arXiv, DOI, GitHub, and generic URLs. This supports source mapping, but user-provided URL locators can trigger outbound HTTP requests and should be gated in restricted environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: HTTP transport and provider-specific external endpoints are confirmed. The code appears to retrieve metadata, but arbitrary URL metadata fetching creates an outbound network surface.

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Checks for Optional Providers
medium_findings.description: The optional provider adapter checks whether API key environment variables exist for OpenRouter, Perplexity, and Parallel. No evidence found that these secrets are transmitted in the placeholder implementation, but the access should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Specific API key variable names and os.environ access are present. The reviewed function returns None and does not send secrets, so the confirmed risk is secret presence detection.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Most Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in Markdown instructions, templates, or Python f-strings that format report text. They are not Ruby command execution evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found these examples are Markdown inline code or generated narrative strings. No Ruby runtime files were identified in the cited paths.

low_findings.title: SHA1 Use Is for Stable Identifiers, Not Cryptographic Trust
low_findings.description: Weak cryptographic algorithm findings map to hashlib.sha1 used for cache keys, filenames, context identifiers, and stable digests. No password hashing, signature validation, or security boundary was found in the reviewed uses.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The static pattern is real, but reviewed contexts use SHA1 for non-adversarial identifiers. This is a false positive for cryptographic security impact unless identifiers become trust boundaries.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are Reference Loading Instructions
low_findings.description: The path traversal findings in SKILL.md are relative references to shared policy documents. They are not code that opens attacker-controlled paths, but they do instruct the agent to read files outside the skill directory.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is instruction text, not filesystem code. The broader-package reference behavior is intentional but should be checked during packaging.

low_findings.title: Base64 Decoding Is GitHub README Handling
low_findings.description: The base64 decode finding is used to decode GitHub API README content before extracting paper links. No hidden payload execution or obfuscated control flow was found in that reviewed code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The GitHub API returns README content as base64, and the decoded text is parsed for URLs. There is no exec or eval path tied to the decoded content.

low_findings.title: Temporary and Artifact File Writes
low_findings.description: The skill writes temporary JSON context files and persistent research artifacts under sources, analysis_outputs, and explore_outputs paths. These writes are expected for the workflow but can change the workspace.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Filesystem writes are directly present and aligned with documented artifact generation. No destructive deletion beyond cleanup of its own temp file was found in the reviewed snippets.

dangerous_patterns.title: Combined Execution, Network, Filesystem, and Environment Surfaces
dangerous_patterns.description: The static dangerous-combination heuristic is partly valid because the skill includes subprocess execution, public HTTP lookup, filesystem writes, and API-key environment checks. AI review did not find credential exfiltration or malicious intent, so this is a medium operational-risk pattern rather than a critical block.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The combined surfaces are confirmed across files. The reviewed code supports research workflow operations and lacks evidence of secret transmission or covert execution.

content.value_statement: Research teams need structured exploration without confusing early candidates with proven results. This skill turns a fixed research anchor into ranked ideas, source evidence, feasibility checks, and auditable outputs.
actual_capabilities: Confirms a durable current_research anchor before exploration begins.
Loads campaign or variant specifications for task, dataset, benchmark, evaluation, and budget constraints.
Runs deterministic passes for source lookup, idea decomposition, candidate generation, ranking, feasibility, and fidelity checks.
Creates candidate-only artifacts under analysis_outputs, sources, and explore_outputs paths.
Can resolve public research metadata from arXiv, DOI, GitHub, and generic URL locators.
Can create an isolated git branch or worktree for bounded experiment work.
limitations: It does not prove novelty, significance, or global benchmark completeness.
It requires an existing current_research anchor and explicit exploration authorization.
It may execute local git, Python, and hardware-detection commands when orchestration is used.
Network source lookup depends on public provider availability and user-approved locators.

use_cases.title: Rank Candidate Research Ideas
use_cases.description: Compare proposed deep learning changes against a fixed task, dataset, evaluation source, and compute budget.

use_cases.title: Prepare Auditable Experiment Plans
use_cases.description: Create bounded implementation and smoke-validation plans before spending compute on candidate variants.

use_cases.title: Review Exploration Evidence
use_cases.description: Inspect source support, ranking gates, comparison limits, and artifact paths before approving the next experiment.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Bounded Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin with a known research anchor.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore with current_research at <branch or commit>. Task family: <task>. Dataset: <dataset>. Benchmark: <benchmark>. Evaluation source: <source>. SOTA reference: <reference>. Compute budget: <budget>.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Provided Ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore to preserve and rank these candidate ideas against my current_research anchor. Keep the evaluation source frozen and explain expected upside, implementation risk, eval risk, and rollback ease.

prompt_templates.title: Add Source-Grounded Exploration
prompt_templates.scenario: Use public locators and local repository evidence.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore with bounded source lookup. Resolve these arXiv, DOI, GitHub, and URL locators, map them to candidate ideas, and mark unsupported claims instead of filling gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan One Executable Candidate
prompt_templates.scenario: Move from ranking to one isolated experiment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ai-research-explore to select one candidate for an isolated branch or worktree. Produce implementation, smoke-validation, comparison, and rollback artifacts without claiming trusted reproduction success.

output_examples.input: A campaign with a fixed image classification benchmark and three candidate architecture changes.
output_examples.output: A ranked candidate list with score breakdowns, source support notes, feasibility risks, and a recommended next experiment.

output_examples.input: A current_research branch plus arXiv, DOI, and GitHub locators for related work.
output_examples.output: A source inventory, mapped evidence classes, unresolved locator notes, and comparison boundaries for each candidate idea.

output_examples.input: A selected candidate with a short-run budget and baseline metric.
output_examples.output: An experiment workspace plan, smoke-validation criteria, artifact locations, and a comparability report outline.

best_practices: Provide a durable current_research branch, commit, checkpoint, or run record before using the skill.
Freeze task, dataset, benchmark, evaluation source, SOTA reference, and compute budget before ranking ideas.
Treat all gains as exploratory until reproduced with fair baselines, ablations, and documented comparison limits.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for open-ended literature search without a fixed research anchor.
Do not use it to claim novelty or SOTA from a single exploratory run.
Do not allow unrestricted execution, network lookup, or workspace mutation without reviewing the campaign scope.

faq.question: Does this skill prove a research idea is novel?
faq.answer: No. It treats novelty as a hypothesis and requires source contrast, ablations, and fair comparison before strong claims.

faq.question: What inputs are required?
faq.answer: A durable current_research anchor and explicit exploration authorization are required. Campaign fields should define task, dataset, benchmark, evaluation, references, and budget.

faq.question: Can it run experiments?
faq.answer: It can orchestrate bounded experiment workflows and smoke checks when authorized. It should not run broad autonomous experiments by default.

faq.question: Does it access the network?
faq.answer: It can use bounded public metadata lookup for arXiv, DOI, GitHub, and URL sources when source lookup is enabled.

faq.question: Can it modify my repository?
faq.answer: It can create git branches or worktrees and write exploration artifacts when orchestration is used. Review the plan before execution.

faq.question: Is it suitable for trusted reproduction?
faq.answer: No. It is for candidate exploration. Trusted reproduction needs a separate reproduction workflow and stronger evidence standards.
